evals.prompt: I have a blog about personal finance with 10K monthly visitors but only 200 email subscribers. How do I grow my list?
evals.expected_output: Recommends lead magnets and opt-in form optimization to convert existing traffic

assertions.name: recommends_lead_magnet
assertions.description: Suggests specific lead magnet types relevant to personal finance (checklist, template, calculator)

assertions.name: opt_in_optimization
assertions.description: Recommends opt-in form placement and copy improvements (exit-intent, inline, specific CTA)

assertions.name: conversion_benchmarks
assertions.description: Provides conversion rate benchmarks so the user knows what good looks like

evals.prompt: How do I set up a subscriber referral program for my newsletter?
evals.expected_output: Referral program design with reward tiers, platform options, and implementation steps

assertions.name: reward_tiers
assertions.description: Suggests a tiered reward structure with specific referral counts and rewards

assertions.name: platform_options
assertions.description: Mentions at least 2 platform options for running referral programs (e.g., Kit Pro, SparkLoop, Beehiiv)

assertions.name: fraud_prevention
assertions.description: Addresses referral fraud or quality concerns

evals.prompt: What's the best cross-promotion strategy for a 3,000-subscriber tech newsletter?
evals.expected_output: Cross-promotion strategy including newsletter swaps, paid recommendations, and partner selection

assertions.name: newsletter_swaps
assertions.description: Explains mutual newsletter swap mechanics and how to find partners

assertions.name: paid_recommendations
assertions.description: Mentions paid recommendation networks (Kit Creator Recs, SparkLoop, Beehiiv Boosts) with pricing

assertions.name: partner_criteria
assertions.description: Provides criteria for selecting good cross-promotion partners (similar size, niche, non-competing)
